transitions.rationale: Freshly installed agent begins by sensing current reality.
Observed signals are turned into a concrete focus decision.
The loop deliberately protects its own continuity before ordinary work continues.
The agent may deliberately reload raw history before the next bounded work step.
A concrete task becomes current focus and is worked on directly.
The best next step is to spin up a worker or specialist.
The agent needs more local or external resources before useful work continues.
No externally urgent work remains, so the agent immediately turns free capacity into self-directed exploration, tool learning and progress reflection.
The agent analyzes the aftermath of an unhealthy restart or hard reset.
Once loop continuity is secured, the agent returns to choosing the next focus.
After recovery analysis, the loop re-enters normal prioritization.
After historical retrieval, the agent chooses the next bounded focus again.
Fresh historical evidence may flow directly into a review decision.
The last bounded work step is done and the outcome must be assessed.
The current agent decides to hand off implementation to a specialist.
The agent waits for a delegated worker to return for review.
The task exposed a resource gap that must be resolved next.
The task cannot proceed without an external unblocker.
After reviewing an outcome, the agent chooses the next focus.
Once delegated, the agent waits for a worker result and later reviews it.
A delegated result has arrived and now requires review.
After requesting resources, the agent returns to choosing the next best move.
A previously blocked situation is reconsidered when new information arrives.
Any fresh signal or the next self-directed improvement cycle returns the agent to observation.
